While the primary concern about neonatal infection does indeed align closely with the last several days before birth due to the higher probability of exposure through active skin lesions, there's another aspect worth considering: immune system maturity just prior to birth. As infants approach their due dates, they develop more robust immune responses capable of managing infections better than those born earlier.
